bug_summary: The per-writer snapshot temp-file name derived uniqueness from pid + SystemTime nanoseconds, but threads in one process share the pid and can read the same nanosecond on a coarse clock, so two concurrent savers collide on one temp path; the first rename consumes the file and the second rename fails with ENOENT, making save() spuriously return Err under concurrency.

cases.title: Snapshot save temp name uses pid+nanos nonce that collides across same-process threads
cases.instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt. Do not claim to have run tests.

cases.title: Atomic write-then-rename uses pid+timestamp temp name that races across threads
cases.instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt. Do not claim to have run tests.
